memory_items.title: Cross-Sheet Lookup with openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: When performing value lookups between sheets, use openpyxl.load_workbook() to build a dictionary map from source columns, then apply replacements via cell iteration.
memory_items.generalization: For multi-sheet operations requiring formula/formatting preservation, prefer openpyxl cell-level manipulation over pandas DataFrame operations.

memory_items.title: Value Type Normalization
memory_items.lesson: Always convert cell values to strings before dictionary lookup to avoid type mismatch failures (e.g., int vs string IDs).
memory_items.generalization: Normalize comparison keys to consistent types during cross-reference operations to prevent silent lookup failures.

memory_items.title: Precision Scope Targeting
memory_items.lesson: Apply changes only to explicitly specified columns/ranges; iterate row-by-row to catch dynamic additions.
memory_items.generalization: Control operation scope tightly to satisfy soft restrictions about not touching unrelated data.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Cross-Sheet Lookups
When replacing values across worksheets using a lookup table:
1. Use `load_workbook()` with openpyxl to preserve formatting and formulas
2. Build a dictionary mapping from source sheet columns first
3. Normalize values to strings before comparison to prevent type mismatches
4. Apply replacements via direct cell iteration (`cell(row, column).value`) for precise scope control
5. Save via `wb.save()` to maintain workbook integrity

### Scope Control Best Practices
- Only modify the exact columns/sheets specified in instructions
- Iterate through all rows (including those beyond initial known data)
- Never add columns or create new tabs unless explicitly required
